name: OpenAI o1
pricing_notes: o1: $15/M input tokens, $60/M output; o1-mini: $1.10/M input, $4.40/M output

performance.notes: Extended thinking (reasoning tokens) adds 5-60s latency vs GPT-4o; p99 varies with problem complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Extended thinking model. No system prompt in early versions (now supported). Tool calling supported. Higher cost vs o3 for similar reasoning performance. Batch API 50% discount. Reasoning effort control (low/medium/high) available.

gotchas: Very expensive: 15x cost vs GPT-4o for input tokens
High latency (5-60s) makes interactive use cases slow
Cannot see reasoning tokens (internal chain-of-thought not exposed)
o3 generally supersedes o1 for most tasks at lower latency
Not ideal for simple/conversational tasks — overkill
best_when: Complex multi-step reasoning requiring reliable correctness (math, code review, analysis)
Batch jobs where latency is acceptable but quality is critical
Tasks where GPT-4o/Claude Sonnet reasoning is insufficient
Scientific or research workflows requiring deep analysis
avoid_when: Simple tasks that don't benefit from extended thinking
Most cases where o3 or o3-mini would suffice
summary: OpenAI o1 was the first 'reasoning' model in the o-series, using extended thinking (internal chain-of-thought). Excellent at complex math, coding, and multi-step analysis. High cost ($15/M input) and latency (5-60s) are primary constraints. Largely superseded by o3 for most reasoning tasks, but o1 remains relevant for specific workloads that benefited from its particular training approach.
